chapter_context.summary: Joab uses a wise woman from Tekoa to persuade David to bring Absalom back from exile; David relents but refuses to see Absalom for two years.

outline.text: Joab Schemes to Bring Absalom Back; Wise Woman of Tekoa
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Joab Schemes to Bring Absalom Back; Wise Woman of Tekoa (14:1-11), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: Woman's Parable Applied to King; David Guesses Joab's Hand
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Woman's Parable Applied to King; David Guesses Joab's Hand (14:12-20), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: David Commands Absalom's Return; Bars Him from Court
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Commands Absalom's Return; Bars Him from Court (14:21-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: Outline of Absalom's Beauty and Hair Weight
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Outline of Absalom's Beauty and Hair Weight (14:25-27), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: Absalom Burns Joab's Field to Get Audience; Restored to David
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Absalom Burns Joab's Field to Get Audience; Restored to David (14:28-33), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

key_themes.title: The Davidic Covenant
key_themes.description: God's unconditional promise of an eternal dynasty, ultimately pointing forward to the Messiah.

key_themes.title: Sin and Consequences
key_themes.description: While God offers complete forgiveness for sincere repentance, the earthly consequences of sin remain.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 14 demonstrate the grace of God in the midst of human failure?
What lessons about leadership and accountability can you apply to your own life from this chapter?

cultural_historical.title: Jerusalem as Capital
cultural_historical.text: By capturing Jerusalem, a neutral city on the border of Judah and Benjamin, David strategically united the fractured northern and southern tribes under a single capital.
